title: GradeResult
description: DATAGLOW's Confidence-Calibrated two-axis grade. Both axes are explicitly HEURISTIC, not legal or clinical determinations. Mirrors the object produced by computeCalibratedGrades() in js/calibrated-grades.js.

packName.description: Domain pack applied when grading (e.g. "healthcare", or "none" for raw domain-agnostic output).

integrity.description: Data Integrity axis — mechanical/statistical well-formedness, domain-agnostic (weighted pass-rate over unit_tests, cross_column_logic, categorical_consistency, schema_fingerprint, sanity_anchor, reproducibility).

plausibility.description: Domain Confidence axis — real-world plausibility (weighted pass-rate over physiological_plausibility, distribution_drift, semantic_drift, outlier_detection, benford, correlation_watchdog), with reinterpretation credit for flags the domain pack contextualised. Field name kept as "plausibility" for wire compatibility.

overall.description: Optional combined single-glance signal — the mean of the integrity and plausibility axis scores, banded to A–F.

grade.description: A–F band, matching the CDC-framework banding used across DATAGLOW.

score.description: Normalized [0,1] heuristic score for this axis.

explanation.description: Human-readable, explicitly-heuristic explanation of how the score was derived.

passed.description: Integrity axis: count of internal-consistency checks that passed.

considered.description: Integrity axis: count of internal-consistency checks that were runnable and counted.

interpreted.description: Plausibility axis: count of raised flags the domain pack reinterpreted in context.

concerns.description: Plausibility axis: count of flags raised by the concern layers.

layers.description: Per-layer contribution breakdown feeding this axis (auditable).
